name: Double-Cheese Double-Baked Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Cut off 1/2 inch from the long side of each potato. Scoop cooked potato into a bowl, leaving a 1/4-inch-thick shell. With a fork or potato masher, mash the cooked potato in the bowl; beat in ricotta, Parmesan, milk, basil, salt, and pepper. Spoon the mixture into the potato shells, dividing evenly and heaping the filling if necessary.
3. Lightly spray a cookie sheet with nonstick cooking spray; place the stuffed potatoes on the cookie sheet. Bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes.
Variation: Halve baked potatoes lengthwise; scoop out pulp, leaving thin shells. Make the filling as above; spoon filling back into each potato half, dividing evenly. If using a microwave instead of baking the stuffed potatoes in a conventional oven, place the potatoes on a microwave serving plate. Microwave on Medium until heated through, about 8 to 10 minutes.
Makes 4 servings. Preparation time: About 15 minutes. Cooking time: About 15 minutes.
Per serving: About 252 calories, 7 grams protein, 35 grams carbohydrates, 7 grams fat, 25% calories from fat, 23 milligrams cholesterol, 478 milligrams sodium, 4 grams fiber.
Busted by Gail Shermeyer
